title: Set the vision
subtitle: Where should the company be in 2027? Swipe the statements that fit.

cards.label: Closing the books in real time, not real weeks.
Fewer late nights, more time for the actual business.
Updates arrive automatically — no more big-bang upgrades.
Decisions based on live data, not last month's export.
A clean core: standard where possible, custom where it counts.

title: The business case in 90 seconds
subtitle: Three quick ones on what the cloud actually buys you.

questions.prompt: What's the biggest reporting shift with S/4HANA Cloud?
questions.explanation: In-memory processing means you analyse live operational data — no waiting for the nightly batch.

questions.prompt: How do upgrades work in the cloud model?
questions.explanation: Regular automatic updates replace the big, risky upgrade projects.

questions.prompt: What does 'clean core' protect you from?

options.label: Custom code that blocks future updates
Having any custom code at all

questions.explanation: Keeping the core clean (extensions, not modifications) is what keeps automatic updates frictionless.

title: Make your commitment
subtitle: Unlock a commitment statement everyone can see.

intro.bot: As Captain, your visible commitment sets the tone. Ready to lock one in?

why.bot: When the CEO commits out loud, departments stop waiting and start moving. That's the whole game. Shall we?

ambition.bot: Pick the tone of your commitment:

options.label: "We move together, no one left behind."

done.bot: Locked in and shared with the crew. The whole company can see the Captain is on board. 🚀

title: What changes with real-time reporting?
subtitle: Two quick ones for the numbers people.

questions.prompt: Today, how fresh is the data in most of your management reports?
Which reconciliation step disappears when finance & controlling share one source of truth?

options.label: Matching FI and CO totals by hand

questions.explanation: The universal journal means FI and CO read the same line items — no more reconciliation between them.

title: Tag the slow reports
subtitle: Swipe the reports that take too long today. (A tiny, real pain-point check.)

payload.prompt: How does this one feel today?

title: Guess the custom-code truth
subtitle: No wrong answers — just a gut check.

questions.prompt: Of all your custom developments, how many do you reckon are actually still used?
questions.explanation: Industry checks routinely find 40–60% of custom code is unused. Migration is the perfect moment to drop the dead weight.

title: Keep or retire?
subtitle: A quick clean-up pass on some custom objects.

payload.prompt: This custom object — bring it along or leave it behind?

cards.label: Pricing routine for a discontinued product

title: Hot, warm or cold?
subtitle: Sort what should come along live — and what can be archived.

cards.label: Employees who left a decade ago

title: Why data quality wins
subtitle: One that every Data-Guardian knows in their bones.

questions.prompt: What's the golden rule of a migration?
questions.explanation: Migrating mess just gives you faster mess. A clean-up pass first pays for itself many times over.

title: Which step annoys you most?
subtitle: Low-barrier process check — pick your nemesis.

questions.prompt: Which process step costs you the most patience today?

options.label: Manual approvals that sit in inboxes

title: Smooth or bottleneck?
subtitle: Swipe each step the way it really feels on the floor.

payload.prompt: On a normal day, this step is...

intro.bot: Quick vibe check — when people hear 'new system coming', the room mostly feels...?

curious.bot: Love that. Curiosity is rocket fuel. What would help them most?

nervous.bot: Totally normal. Nervous usually means 'I don't know what changes for me yet'. What would calm it?

tired.bot: Heard. Change fatigue is real. The trick is making this one feel different — lighter, with quick wins.

done.bot: Logged. You just turned a feeling into a signal the whole team can act on. 💛

title: What helps change land?
subtitle: One quick one on the human side.

questions.prompt: What predicts a smooth rollout best?
questions.explanation: Adoption beats tooling. Teams that feel involved adopt faster and complain less.
